what_it_does: Defines a React InspectWorkspace for close visual Q&A on one selected mockup, with thumbnail selection, image hotspots, region readouts, flagging, a compare overlay, and scripted Vi question/answer interactions.
observations: Reads the active sequence, candidate mockups, an approved mockup with id m_ci_v2, and builds a selectable pool from them.
Tracks focused mockup, selected region, flagged regions, compare mode, and local Q&A thread in component state.
Sets window.ViContext for workspace inspect, the active sequence, state st_05, and the focused mockup when the focused mockup changes.
Resets the Q&A thread and compare mode when the focused mockup changes while preserving the selected region.
Renders selectable region hotspots over the focused mockup using file-local REGIONS coordinates and metadata.
Compare mode overlays the approved mockup over half of the focused image and hides hotspots while active.
The right panel presents the selected region role, tone, read text, flag action, scripted questions, scripted answers, and a Vi recommendation note.
REGIONS, QUESTIONS, and ANSWERS provide the file-local interpretation data driving the UI copy and interactions.
standouts: The region model combines visual coordinates, semantic role, tone, label, and interpretive read text in one local structure.
The interaction loop is region-centered: select hotspot, read interpretation, ask scripted questions, optionally flag.
Flag state directly affects hotspot pins, region strip status dots, the region button, and the build handoff CTA.
conventions: Uses global React bindings and assigns the component to window.InspectWorkspace.
Relies on global data and components such as window.MS_DATA, window.ViContext, Btn, Panel, Chip, Icon, MockThumb, ViNote, and ViMark.
Uses inline styles heavily for layout, positioning, and small visual treatments.
Keeps UI data constants in the same file as the component.
Uses object state keyed by region id for flags and appends Q&A messages immutably.
concerns: The initial focusedId assumes candidates[2] exists.
The approved mockup lookup assumes an item with id m_ci_v2 exists.
The effect that writes ViContext depends only on focusedId while reading seq, data.states, and focused.
ask(q) assumes region is defined when accessing region.id and region.label.
The Q&A thread can accumulate repeated identical prompts without deduplication or limits.
notes: This file is a self-contained visual inspection workspace where a mockup image is interrogated through predefined semantic regions, scripted Vi reads, and flaggable build handoff signals.
questions: What should happen if there are fewer than three candidate mockups?
What should happen if the approved mockup id m_ci_v2 is absent?
Should flagged regions persist across focused mockup changes or be scoped per mockup?
Should scripted question clicks that imply flagging also update the flags state?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: the file provides a slow close-read workspace for selecting mockup regions, receiving Vi-style interpretation, comparing to an approved frame, asking region-specific questions, and flagging issues for build.
intention_signals: It seems intended to turn a static mockup into an inspectable design substrate where visible regions carry semantic roles, rule checks, warnings, and actionable flags.
novelty: The file treats image hotspots as interpretive design objects rather than simple navigation controls, pairing each region with role, tone, read text, suggested questions, and build handoff state.
contribution: Provides a region-based inspection pattern that preserves image-space selection, semantic annotations, warning/holding tones, scripted inquiry, compare overlay behavior, and flag handoff from visual review into downstream work.
summary_for_embedding: React workspace for inspecting one mockup through selectable image regions, Vi-style region interpretations, scripted Q&A, compare overlay, and flagging for build handoff.
intention_for_embedding: This was for close-reading a candidate visual mockup before specification by mapping visual regions to design roles, rule checks, questions, and actionable flags.

symbols.does: Adds a question and generated answer pair to the local Q&A thread for the currently selected region.
symbols.description: The function looks up a scripted answer by selected region id and question text, falling back to a generic region-specific answer when none is found. It appends the question and answer to thread state without changing region, flag, or compare state.

symbols.does: Toggles the boolean flag state for a given region id.
symbols.description: The function updates the flags object immutably by copying existing entries and inverting the selected id. That state is then used by the hotspot pin, region strip dot, selected-region flag button, flag count, and build handoff CTA.
